mission.title: Install and Configure Ollama
mission.description: Ollama is an AI platform designed for serving large language models (LLMs) such as Kimi-K2.5 and Gemma. It provides a simple interface to run and manage models, making it suitable for developers looking to integrate AI capabilities into their applications. Key trade-offs include ease of use versus the flexibility of more complex AI frameworks.

steps.title: Install the Ollama CLI
steps.description:
This step installs the Ollama command-line interface, which is necessary to interact with the Ollama platform.

```bash

curl -fsSL https://ollama.com/install.sh | sh

irm https://ollama.com/install.ps1 | iex

```

> Use this command for Linux and macOS.
> Use this command for Windows.

steps.title: Create a Kubernetes Cluster
steps.description:
This step creates a new Kubernetes cluster where Ollama will be deployed. Adjust the node count and region as needed.

```bash

gcloud container clusters create ollama-cluster --num-nodes=3 --zone=us-central1-a --cluster-version=1.30

```

> Ensure you have gcloud CLI installed and authenticated.

steps.title: Set Up Kubeconfig
steps.description:
This step configures your local kubeconfig to interact with the newly created cluster.

```bash

gcloud container clusters get-credentials ollama-cluster --zone=us-central1-a

```

> This command sets up your kubeconfig.

steps.title: Deploy Ollama on Kubernetes
steps.description:
This step deploys the Ollama application on the Kubernetes cluster using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add ollama https://ollama.com/charts

helm install ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama --create-namespace

```

> Add the Ollama Helm repository.
> Install Ollama with a specific version.

steps.title: Verify the Deployment
steps.description:
This step checks that the Ollama pods are running correctly in the cluster.

```bash

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Ensure all pods are in the 'Running' state.

steps.title: Post-Install Configuration
steps.description:
This step sets up additional configurations such as autoscaling and monitoring for Ollama.

```bash

kubectl autoscale deployment ollama --cpu-percent=50 --min=1 --max=10 -n ollama

```

> Set up autoscaling based on CPU usage.

uninstall.title: Uninstall Ollama
uninstall.description:
This step removes the Ollama deployment from your Kubernetes cluster. Be aware that this will delete all associated resources.

```bash

helm uninstall ollama --namespace ollama

gcloud container clusters delete ollama-cluster --zone=us-central1-a --quiet

```

> This command will remove the Ollama deployment.
> This command will delete the entire cluster and all resources.

upgrade.title: Upgrade Ollama
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you back up any necessary data. This step upgrades the Ollama deployment to the latest version.

```bash

kubectl drain <node-name> --ignore-daemonsets

helm upgrade ollama ollama/ollama --version 0.17.7 --namespace ollama

kubectl get pods -n ollama

```

> Drain the node before upgrading.
> Upgrade to the specified version.
> Verify that the pods are running after the upgrade.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can occur if the image is not available or the image name is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it exists in the registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Pod is in CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the application is failing to start properly.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs with 'kubectl logs <pod-name> -n ollama' for more details.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient CPU resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This indicates that the cluster does not have enough resources to schedule the pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the node count or resize the existing nodes.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Connection refused'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** This can happen if the service is not running or not exposed correctly.

**Fix:**
Ensure the service is running and check the service configuration.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models including MiniMax and DeepSeek, improved API response times.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: No specific migration steps required for this version.

resolution.summary: You should see output from 'kubectl get pods -n ollama' showing all pods in the 'Running' state.

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have the necessary tools and resources before starting the installation.
